data.source: FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims) via fred_series adapter

behavior: Weekly jobless claims spike above 4-week moving average by >15%, historically followed by 1-2 week relief rally as traders price in temporary shock.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer discretionary rallies on claims relief after initial panic, as jobs data stabilizes expectations.

trade_logic.entry: If ICSA > (4-week MA + 15%), enter long XLY
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days OR if ICSA drops below 2-week MA

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Claims data is weekly; seasonal volatility and economic noise ensure 2–3 spikes per month above threshold.

data.source: FRED series TRUCKD (Truck Tonnage Index) via fred_series adapter

behavior: Truck tonnage falls >3% week-over-week, signaling logistics slowdown. Historically mean-reverts within 2–3 weeks as demand reasserts.

market_impact.rationale: Industrial/transport stocks fall on demand shock; rebound trades when tonnage stabilizes.

trade_logic.entry: If TRUCKD drops >3% WoW and 5-day RSI < 35, short XLI
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days OR if TRUCKD posts 2 consecutive WoW gains

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Weekly tonnage data swings >3% regularly due to holiday, seasonal, and shipping-cycle volatility.

data.source: FRED series DHHNGSP (Henry Hub Natural Gas Spot Price) + Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly

behavior: When US avg temperature drops >2 standard deviations below normal and gas prices lag spot moves, heating demand surge is priced in slowly. Utilities and energy stocks get whipsawed.

market_impact.rationale: Utilities benefit from heating demand; price reaction lags weather, creating mean-reversion entry.

trade_logic.entry: If daily US avg temp anomaly < -2 SD and XLU underperforms XLP by >1.5% over 3 days, long XLU
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days OR once temp anomaly crosses back to -1 SD

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Winter/early spring weather swings >2 SD occur multiple times per month; daily data fires frequently.

data.source: OpenChargeMap public charger count API via openchargemap adapter, aggregated by state weekly

behavior: Weekly net additions of public EV chargers in top 5 states surge >20% above 12-week rolling average, signaling infrastructure grant deployment or corporate spending wave.

market_impact.rationale: EV buildout indirectly bullish for energy infrastructure, materials (XLB), and industrial (XLI) as capex ramps.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly net charger adds > (12-week MA + 20%), long XLI (industrial/materials suppliers)
trade_logic.exit: After 14 trading days OR if adds fall below 12-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Infrastructure spending cycles and seasonal capex deployment ensure 2–3 spikes per quarter above threshold.

data.source: BTS airline load factor weekly via bts_airline_load_factor adapter

behavior: Weekly load factor drops >2.5 percentage points from prior week (e.g., 82% to 79.5%), signaling demand shock. Historically bounces within 3 weeks as pricing adjusts.

market_impact.rationale: Airlines and hospitality compress margins; consumer discretionary sentiment falls on travel weakness.

trade_logic.entry: If load factor WoW drop > 2.5 ppts, short XLY for 7 trading days
trade_logic.exit: After 7 days OR if load factor rebounds >1.5 ppts WoW

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal demand swings, holidays, and fuel/economic shocks cause load factor whipsaws weekly.

idea: Container Port Volume Spike-Drop Friction Trade

data.source: Port container volume aggregate (Port of Long Beach, Port of NY/NJ) via port_container_volume adapter, weekly

behavior: Weekly container volume (TEU) swings >8% from 4-week MA signal logistics bottleneck or surge. Retail/industrial stocks gap on shipping friction expectations.

market_impact.rationale: Sharp volume swings signal supply-chain friction; industrial and materials stocks react negatively to logistics volatility.

trade_logic.entry: If weekly TEU > (4-week MA + 8%) OR < (4-week MA - 8%), short XLI
trade_logic.exit: After 10 trading days OR if TEU returns within 3% of 4-week MA

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal shipping cycles, holidays, and demand shocks drive ±8% swings monthly.

idea: Healthcare Cost Shock Google Search Spike Trade

data.source: Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical bill debt' + 'insurance deductible shock' via google_trends adapter

behavior: Combined search volume for medical cost distress terms spikes >40% above 12-week MA, indicating acute consumer stress. Staples and defensive stocks rally as discretionary demand falters.

market_impact.rationale: Consumer stress rotation favors staples; healthcare cost anxiety is sticky and reverses slowly, supporting defensive positioning.

trade_logic.entry: If combined search volume > (12-week MA + 40%), long XLP
trade_logic.exit: After 14 trading days OR if search volume falls below 12-week MA

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Seasonal billing cycles, insurance deductible resets, and healthcare premium announcements cause monthly spikes.

idea: Earthquake Activity Precursor Insurance & Construction Volatility

data.source: USGS earthquake activity magnitude >4.0 US daily count via earthquake_activity adapter

behavior: Daily count of US earthquakes >4.0 magnitude spikes >3 days running above 12-month average, triggering insurance & construction cost anxiety. Financials (insurance) and materials (rebuilding) gap.

market_impact.rationale: Elevated seismic activity increases insurer loss reserves and risk perception; financial sector reprices catastrophe risk.

trade_logic.entry: If 3-day rolling count of mag >4.0 events > 12-month daily avg, short XLF
trade_logic.exit: After 7 trading days OR if 7-day rolling count drops below 12-month avg

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: US seismic zones (California, Oklahoma, Pacific NW) experience clusters monthly; daily data ensures frequent firing.

idea: Fed Liquidity Drain Week Volatility Squeeze

data.source: FRED series WALCL (Federal Reserve Assets, Liabilities) via fred_series adapter, weekly change

behavior: Weekly Fed balance sheet shrinkage >$5B signals liquidity drain. Historically correlates with intraweek volatility spike and defensive sector outperformance.

market_impact.rationale: Liquidity tightening favors stable dividend payers (utilities) over growth stocks; flight to safety.

trade_logic.entry: If WALCL weekly change < -$5B, long XLU vs. XLK spread (long XLU, short XLK)
trade_logic.exit: After 5 trading days OR if WALCL rebounds >$2B WoW

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Fed quantitative tightening is structural; weekly >$5B drains occur regularly during rate-hike cycles.

data.source: FRED series LMNRNRNR (Banks Tightening Lending Standards Net Percentage) via fred_series adapter, quarterly proxy to weekly moving average
data.exact_frequency_note: Quarterly release, interpolated to weekly trend

behavior: Lending standards tighten sharply (spike in net % tightening), signaling credit shock. Consumer discretionary and small-cap indices fall as credit availability shrinks.

market_impact.rationale: Tight lending standards crush consumer credit-dependent spending; discretionary retail and finance compress.

trade_logic.entry: If net tightening % > (prior 2-quarter avg + 15 ppts), short XLY for 14 days
trade_logic.exit: After 14 trading days OR if tightening reverses >10 ppts in next quarterly release

reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days: Quarterly Fed surveys release, interpolated weekly; 2–3 sharp tightening shocks per year align with credit events.
